response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a seasoned generalist software developer with 15+ years of experience, but he does not meet the most critical hard requirements for this role. Most disqualifying is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency — the role explicitly requires native-level Japanese, and his listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond that, his technical background is heavily web/TypeScript/Node.js-focused with no demonstrated experience in network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), L2/L3 networking, or embedded C development at a level appropriate for LAN-switch firmware. His C exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and some embedded systems
Demonstrated Linux background and DevOps/infrastructure experience (CI servers, Docker, GitLab)
Brief C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows, Sound Position Server thesis project) shows some low-level programming familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
No experience with network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange) or L2/L3 networking
Primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang; C proficiency for embedded UNIX development is minimal and dated
